title: Playtest checklist and tester protocol
summary: Create the M7 tester protocol and checklist for Windows controlled external test and Web demo-only evaluation after all prerequisite readiness packages are verified.
acceptance: Produces a tester protocol and checklist for Windows controlled external test and Web demo-only evaluation
Includes tutorial, balance, privacy/diagnostics, cultural-risk, save-policy, known-issues and feedback checks
Distinguishes controlled external testing from public release or Early Access launch
Adds an automated checklist validation when feasible, or records why a docs-only checklist is sufficient
Does not execute public recruitment, public launch, store submission, signing, telemetry, accounts or paid services

history.details: Created by WM-0098 M7 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Make M7 external testing repeatable, bounded and evidence-producing without launching publicly.
forbiddenPaths: public release upload store submission signing installer or updater
public recruitment campaign or public Web launch
telemetry accounts paid services crash upload or public feedback service
final privacy legal store or public save compatibility claims
runtime behavior changes unless separately approved
M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: tester protocol can become public launch instructions; keep audience and distribution controlled.
Risk: checklist omits culture/privacy/save blockers; dependencies must be cited explicitly.
File ownership is limited to playtest checklist/protocol docs, optional validation tooling and WM-0108 reports.
ownerGate: Block on public recruitment, public launch, store submission, signing or telemetry
Block if prerequisite cultural/privacy/save/Web/Windows evidence is missing
Block on final privacy/legal/store/save commitments without owner approval
m0M6RegressionCoverage: ci:local must pass with temporary artifact root when running integration-like gates
Checklist must include M5 hash, M6 Web verdict and M6 Windows verdict protections
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline change expected. If checklist validation adds tooling, it must not affect benchmark artifacts.
reviewRouting: qa-performance completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies checklist coverage, dependency citations and no public launch action.
rollbackModel: Revert checklist/protocol docs, optional validation tool and WM-0108 reports; leave upstream evidence intact.

sparkEligibility.reason: Playtest protocol consolidates multiple owner-gated readiness areas and public-facing boundaries.
